entries.definition:
كتاب الجيم

جند - جنز

(جنف) الجيم والنون والفاء أصلٌ واحد وهو المَيْل. يقال جَنِفَ* إذا عَدَل وجار. قال الله تعالى جَلَّ ثناؤه: ] فَمَنْ خَافَ مِنْ مُوصٍ جَنَفَاً [ [البقرة 182]. ورجلٌ أجْنَفُ إذا كان في خَلْقهِ مَيَلٌ، ويقال لا يكون ذلك إلاّ في الطُّول والانحناء. ويقال تجانَفَ عن كذا، إذا مال. قال:

وما عَدَلَتْ عن أهْلِهَا لِسِوائِكا

تَجَانَفُ عَنْ جُلِّ اليَمَامَةِ ناقتي

(باب الجيم والهاء وما يثلثهما)
entries.definition_english:
### Core Semantic Principles
The root **J-N-F** (ج ن ف) establishes a single fundamental meaning, which is **inclination** or **deviation** (*al-mayl*).

### Derivations & Evidence
*   **janifa**: To incline or deviate, specifically implying an act of injustice or swerving from what is right.
    *   Example: It is said, "**janifa** (he inclined/deviated) when he deviated [from justice] and acted unjustly."
    *   Quranic Verse: Allah, exalted be His praise, says: "But whoever fears from an executor **a deviation** (*janafan*)..." [Quran 2:182]. (This verse uses *janafan* to refer to an unjust inclination or bias on the part of an executor of a will.)
*   **ajnaf**: A man characterized by an inclination or deviation in his physical form.
    *   Example: "And a man is **ajnaf** if there is an inclination in his physical form. It is said that this only occurs in height and curvature."
*   **tajānafa ʿan**: To incline or lean away from something.
    *   Example: "And it is said, **tajānafa** ʿan kadhā (he inclined away from such-and-such) when he inclined."
    *   Poetic Verse:
        "My she-camel **inclines away** (*tajānafu*) from the whole of al-Yamāmah,
        And did not deviate from its people for your sake."
        (The poem illustrates *tajānafu* as the act of the she-camel turning or deviating from a specific path or place.)

entries.definition:
j–n–f hunchback, to incline, to deviate, to go over, to be crooked, crookedness. Of this root, two forms occur once each in the Qurʾan: جَنَف janaf and مُتَجَانِف mutajānif.
جَنَف janaf [n./v. n.] unfairness, straying away from the right path, deviation, mistake (2:182) فَمَنْ خَافَ مِنْ مُوصٍ جَنَفًا أَوْ إِثْمًا but if anyone has reason to suspect that the testator has made a mistake, or done wrong.

مُتَجَانِف mutajānif [act. part.] one who inclines towards (5:3) فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ فِي مَخْمَصَةٍ غَيْرَ مُتَجَانِفٍ لِإِثْمٍ but if any one is forced by hunger [to eat such food], with no inclination to sin, then God is most forgiving and merciful.
